texto: I used to think if I could be free I should be the happiest woman," a young Mississippi woman recalled. "But when my master come to me, and says 'Lizzie, you is free!' it seems like I was in a kind of daze. And when I would wake up in the morning I would think to myself, Is I free? Hasn't I got to get up before daylight and go into the field and work?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Leon F. Litwack: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “I used to think if I could be free I should be the happiest woman," a young Mississippi woman rec…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, alavert, forced-labor pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de bom-dia e trabalho.

frase_original: I used to think if I could be free I should be the happiest woman," a young Mississippi woman recalled. "But when my master come to me, and says 'Lizzie, you is free!' it seems like I was in a kind of daze. And when I would wake up in the morning I would think to myself, Is I free? Hasn't I got to get up before daylight and go into the field and work?
